chapter.title: Building the &osips; Rust Module
chapter.para: This guide covers building the Rust module from scratch, whether starting from a bare Debian system, an &osips; Docker container, or an existing &osips; installation.

section.title: Architecture Overview
section.para: The module ships as a Cargo workspace with two crates:
section.programlisting: opensips-rust/ modules/ rust/ opensips-rs/ # Reusable SDK (generates FFI bindings, C shim) src/ # Module (rate limiter, cache, HTTP pool)
section.para: Why Cargo, not Make? &osips;'s build system compiles C modules using make -C modules/<name>. It discovers .c files, compiles them to .o, and links into a .so. A Rust module cannot use this directly because:

listitem.para: Rust code is compiled by rustc via Cargo, not cc.
The SDK uses bindgen to generate FFI bindings at build time.
Dependencies (e.g., reqwest for &http;) are managed by Cargo.

section.para: Two build approaches are supported:

row.entry: Symlink into modules/, make modules module=rust

section.para: Both produce an identical rust.so.

section.title: &osips; Source Tree
section.para: The Rust module compiles against the &osips; C headers. You need the source tree of the same &osips; version you are running. A package install (apt install opensips) is not sufficient — the headers and Makefile infrastructure are not included in binary packages.

row.entry: Required by bindgen for parsing C headers

section.title: Rust Toolchain
section.para: Minimum Rust version: 1.70 (for OnceLock stabilization). Recommended: latest stable via rustup.

section.title: Docker: Build from Scratch
section.para: The official opensips/opensips Docker image installs from .deb packages and does not include headers or source. For a Rust module, use a custom multi-stage Dockerfile that builds everything from source.

section.title: Using the Provided Dockerfile
section.programlisting:
# Build the image (includes OpenSIPS + Rust module) docker build -t opensips-rust .
# Run with a custom config docker run -v ./modules/rust/doc/opensips.cfg.example:/etc/opensips/opensips.cfg \ -p 5060:5060/udp opensips-rust

section.title: Dockerfile Walkthrough
section.para: The provided Dockerfile has three stages:

listitem.para: builder-opensips — Clones and builds &osips; from source. This produces the binary, module .so files, and leaves the source tree intact for the Rust module's bindgen to use.
builder-rust — Installs the Rust toolchain, copies the module source, and runs cargo build --release. The build.rs scripts automatically detect the &osips; version from the source tree.
runtime — Minimal Debian image with only the &osips; binary, core modules, the Rust module, and runtime libraries. No compilers, no source code, no Rust toolchain.

section.xreflabel: Adding Rust to an Existing Image
section.title: Adding Rust to an Existing &osips; Docker Image
section.para: If you already have a Docker image with &osips; built from source, add these layers:
section.programlisting:
# Install Rust build dependencies RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \ pkg-config libssl-dev clang llvm-dev libclang-dev curl ca-certificates
# Install Rust toolchain RUN curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh -s -- -y ENV PATH="/root/.cargo/bin:${PATH}"
# Build the module # OPENSIPS_SRC_DIR must point to the OpenSIPS source tree COPY opensips-rust/ /build/opensips-rust/ RUN cd /build/opensips-rust \ && OPENSIPS_SRC_DIR=/usr/local/src/opensips cargo build --release
# Deploy RUN cp /build/opensips-rust/target/release/libopensips_mod_rust.so \ /usr/local/lib64/opensips/modules/rust.so
section.para: Key requirement: OPENSIPS_SRC_DIR must point to the same source tree that produced the running &osips; binary. Version mismatch = module will not load.

section.title: Step 1: Build &osips; from Source
section.para: Skip this if &osips; is already built and the source tree exists.
section.programlisting:
# Install OpenSIPS build dependencies sudo apt-get update sudo apt-get install -y build-essential bison flex \ libxml2-dev libpcre2-dev pkg-config
# Get the source (use the version matching your deployment) cd /usr/local/src git clone https://github.com/OpenSIPS/opensips.git cd opensips git checkout 3.5 # or master for 4.0-dev
# Build and install make all sudo make install
# Verify /usr/local/sbin/opensips -V
section.para: The source tree at /usr/local/src/opensips/ must remain intact — the Rust module's build system reads Makefile.defs and runs make -n -B against it to extract compiler flags.

section.title: Step 2: Install Rust Toolchain
section.programlisting: curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh -s -- -y source ~/.cargo/env rustc --version # should print 1.70.0 or higher

section.title: Step 3: Install Rust Module Build Dependencies
section.programlisting: sudo apt-get install -y pkg-config libssl-dev clang llvm-dev libclang-dev
section.para: These are needed by:

listitem.para: bindgen (clang, llvm-dev, libclang-dev) — parses &osips; C headers.
reqwest (libssl-dev, pkg-config) — &http; client with TLS support.

section.title: Step 4: Clone and Build the Module
section.programlisting:
git clone <your-repo-url>/opensips-rust.git cd opensips-rust
# Set the OpenSIPS source path (default: /usr/local/src/opensips) export OPENSIPS_SRC_DIR=/usr/local/src/opensips
# Build cargo build --release
section.para: The build takes approximately 2–4 minutes on first run (downloads and compiles dependencies). Subsequent builds take approximately 10–30 seconds.

section.title: Step 5: Deploy
section.programlisting: # Find your modules directory MODDIR=$(/usr/local/sbin/opensips -V 2>&1 \ | grep -oP 'modules.*?/' \ || echo "/usr/local/lib64/opensips/modules/") sudo cp target/release/libopensips_mod_rust.so ${MODDIR}rust.so
section.para: Or use the provided script:
section.programlisting: ./scripts/build.sh # builds and copies to /usr/local/lib64/opensips/modules/

section.title: &osips; Make System Integration
section.para: For production deployments, the module can be integrated into &osips;'s make modules system. This follows the same pattern used by tls_wolfssl, which builds an external C library (wolfSSL via autotools) as a Make dependency.

listitem.para: Create modules/rust/ in the &osips; source tree.
A Makefile there declares the Cargo-built static library as a DEPS.
make modules module=rust invokes Cargo, then links the result.

section.title: Setup
section.programlisting: ./scripts/intree-install.sh /usr/local/src/opensips
section.para: This creates modules/rust/ in the &osips; source tree with:

listitem.para: Makefile — build rules that invoke Cargo.
rust.c — minimal C stub (required by Makefile.sources).
workspace — symlink to the Cargo workspace root.

section.title: Building via Make
section.programlisting: cd /usr/local/src/opensips make modules module=rust
section.para: This will:

listitem.para: Run cargo build --release in the Rust workspace.
Compile rust.c (thin C entry point) with &osips;'s flags.
Link both into modules/rust/rust.so.

section.title: Installing via Make
section.programlisting: sudo make install-modules module=rust

section.title: The In-Tree Makefile Explained
section.programlisting:
include ../../Makefile.defs auto_gen= NAME=rust.so
RUST_DIR=$(shell pwd)/rust RUST_TARGET=$(RUST_DIR)/target/release RUST_LIB=$(RUST_TARGET)/libopensips_mod_rust.a
# --whole-archive forces the linker to include all symbols from the # Rust static library, even though nothing in rust.c references # them. Without this, the `exports` symbol (defined in Rust) would be # discarded and OpenSIPS could not load the module. LIBS += -Wl,--whole-archive $(RUST_LIB) -Wl,--no-whole-archive \ -lpthread -ldl -lm -lssl -lcrypto -lgcc_s DEPS += $(RUST_LIB)
# CRITICAL: include Makefile.modules BEFORE defining custom targets. # Makefile.modules includes Makefile.rules which sets `all` as the # default target. Custom rules defined above the include would become # the default goal, causing make to build only the .a (not the .so). include ../../Makefile.modules
$(RUST_LIB): $(wildcard $(RUST_DIR)/modules/rust/opensips-rs/src/*.rs) cd $(RUST_DIR) && OPENSIPS_SRC_DIR=$(shell cd ../.. && pwd) \ cargo build --release --lib -p opensips-mod-rust
clean: clean-rust
.PHONY: clean-rust clean-rust: -@cd $(RUST_DIR) && cargo clean 2>/dev/null

section.title: The C Entry Point
section.para: rust.c is a minimal file that exists only so &osips;'s Makefile.sources (which does $(wildcard *.c)) finds at least one C file. The actual module exports are defined in Rust:
section.programlisting:
/* rust.c -- Stub for OpenSIPS make system integration. * * The real module_exports struct is defined in Rust (lib.rs) and * exported as a #[no_mangle] static. This file exists so that * Makefile.sources finds a .c file to compile, satisfying the * build system's expectations. * * It compiles to an empty .o that gets linked with the Rust * static library into the final rust.so. */
/* intentionally empty -- all logic lives in Rust */
section.para: Note: The Cargo.toml for opensips-mod-rust must set crate-type = ["staticlib"] instead of ["cdylib"]. The provided modules/rust/ directory shows this change.

section.title: Module Path
section.para: &osips; looks for modules in its configured mpath. Common locations:

listitem.para: Cargo produces: libopensips_mod_rust.so (cdylib) or libopensips_mod_rust.a (staticlib).
&osips; expects: rust.so.
The build.sh script handles the rename automatically.

section.title: Permissions
section.para: The .so file needs to be readable by the &osips; process:
section.programlisting: sudo chmod 644 /usr/local/lib64/opensips/modules/rust.so

section.title: Configuration
section.para: Add to your opensips.cfg:
section.programlisting:
# Module path (adjust for your installation) mpath="/usr/local/lib64/opensips/modules/"
# Load required protocol and signaling modules loadmodule "proto_udp.so" loadmodule "signaling.so" loadmodule "sl.so" loadmodule "tm.so"
# Load the Rust module loadmodule "rust.so"
# Configure parameters (all optional, shown with defaults) modparam("rust", "max_rate", 100) # requests per window modparam("rust", "window_seconds", 60) # rate limit window modparam("rust", "cache_ttl", 300) # cache entry lifetime modparam("rust", "http_timeout", 2) # HTTP timeout in seconds modparam("rust", "pool_size", 4) # HTTP connection pool size
section.para: See modules/rust/doc/opensips.cfg.example for a complete working configuration.

section.title: Config Check
section.programlisting: # Syntax check -- verifies the module loads and all functions resolve /usr/local/sbin/opensips -c -f /path/to/opensips.cfg
section.para: A successful check prints config file ok, exiting....

section.title: Runtime Check
section.programlisting:
# Start OpenSIPS /usr/local/sbin/opensips -f /path/to/opensips.cfg
# Send a test SIP message sipsak -s sip:test@127.0.0.1:5060
# Check logs for module output grep "rust" /var/log/syslog
section.para: You should see:
section.programlisting: rust: module initialized (v4.0) rust: child_init called for rank ... rust: HTTP connection pool initialized (timeout=2s, pool=4)

section.title: Run the Test Suite
section.programlisting: ./scripts/test.sh # builds, deploys, runs opensips -c

section.title: "module version mismatch"
section.para: The module's compiled version strings do not match the running &osips; binary. This happens when:

listitem.para: You built the module against a different source tree than the binary.
The source tree was updated but the module was not rebuilt.

section.para: Fix: rebuild the module against the correct source tree:
section.programlisting: export OPENSIPS_SRC_DIR=/path/to/correct/opensips/source cargo clean && cargo build --release

section.title: "cannot open shared object file"
section.para: The .so is not in the mpath directory. Check:
section.programlisting:
# What mpath is configured? grep mpath /path/to/opensips.cfg
# Is the file there? ls -la /usr/local/lib64/opensips/modules/rust.so

section.xreflabel: bindgen fails with clang not found
section.title: bindgen fails with "clang not found"
section.programlisting: sudo apt-get install clang llvm-dev libclang-dev

section.title: "&osips; source not found"
section.para: Set OPENSIPS_SRC_DIR to point to the &osips; source tree:
section.programlisting: export OPENSIPS_SRC_DIR=/usr/local/src/opensips
section.para: The directory must contain Makefile.defs.

section.xreflabel: Cargo build fails with SSL errors
section.title: Cargo build fails with SSL errors
section.programlisting: sudo apt-get install pkg-config libssl-dev

section.title: "make -n -B" produces no output
section.para: The &osips; source tree needs to have been configured. Run make once (even if it fails) to generate Makefile.conf:
section.programlisting: cd /usr/local/src/opensips && make menuconfig # or just: cp Makefile.conf.template Makefile.conf
